CZ participated in development work at multiple startups from an early age, and Bloomberg was the largest corporation he ever worked for. He admitted that large companies feature rigid bureaucratic systems and fixed rules, and his personality is unsuitable for stable 9-to-5 corporate jobs, making it impossible for him to stay long-term. text: AI's Economics Don't Make Sense AI's monthly subscription model is a scam, companies like OpenAI hid the true per-token costs behind flat fees, letting users burn $8-13 in compute for every $1 of subscription This trained everyone to use these products in ways that are economically impossible to sustain. GitHub Copilot's June 2026 shift to usage-based pricing is the first domino: Microsoft, the best-capitalized player, can no longer eat the losses, and once Anthropic/OpenAI follow suit on consumer tiers, the hype collapses because real costs are brutal The data center math is worse. A 100MW facility runs ~5% gross margin before debt, then goes negative. Stargate Abilene is years behind schedule, and Oracle needs OpenAI to raise/earn $852B by 2030 to avoid collapse Bottom line: the economics only work if tokens get cheaper and AI labs become profitable. Neither is happening.

text: Trump Admin to Unveil Strategic Bitcoin Reserve Plan Soon The Trump administration is actively advancing its Strategic Bitcoin Reserve plan. According to a Bybit weekly report, the White House confirmed it plans to release the formal strategy within the next two months, aiming to utilize approximately 200,000 BTC seized in government enforcement actions as a national reserve asset. Concurrently, White House digital asset advisor Patrick Witt revealed at the Bitcoin 2026 conference that a major announcement regarding the reserve is expected in the coming weeks, with legal and legislative preparations currently underway. text: Aave: DeFi United Releases Plan to Restore KelpDAO rsETH Backing According to Aave, DeFi United released a plan to restore KelpDAO rsETH backing after the April 18 bridge incident, which caused 116,500 rsETH to be abnormally released from the Ethereum-side adapter. The plan will use committed ETH funding to refill the affected lockbox and temporarily adjust the rsETH oracle price through governance to clear exploiter-related positions on Aave and Compound. WETH and rsETH markets across multiple chains will remain frozen until normal operations resume. — link

text: Arthur Hayes: War Spending to Drive Bitcoin to $125K by Year-End At Bitcoin Vegas 2026, BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes predicted BTC will reach $125,000 by year-end, driven by massive liquidity from wartime defense spending and U.S. banking deregulation. Hayes noted the market narrative has shifted from AI-driven "credit deflation" to wartime inflation. Furthermore, he estimated that the recent Enhanced Supplemental Leverage Ratio (ESLR) adjustment could unlock $1.3 trillion in new loans, potentially generating up to $4 trillion in total credit creation to boost financial markets. — link

text: Colombia's Largest Pension Fund Moves Into Bitcoin via BlackRock's IBIT According to CriptoNoticias, Colombia's largest pension fund manager Porvenir has launched a crypto portfolio that offers indirect Bitcoin exposure via IBIT. The product is limited to voluntary pension accounts, with a minimum investment of COP 100,000 (~$25), and requires risk profiling before participation. Local pension managers Protección and Skandia have previously introduced similar offerings, positioning crypto as a long-term portfolio diversification tool. — link
